title: Password Hashing with bcrypt
description: Store passwords securely using hashing and salting.

slides.title: Why Hash Passwords? 🔒
slides.content:
# Never Store Plain Text Passwords

If a database is breached, plain text passwords are immediately exposed. Users often reuse passwords across sites – a disaster.

**Solution:** Hash the password before storing. Hashing is a one-way function; you cannot reverse it to get the original password.

But simple hashing (like SHA-256) is vulnerable to **rainbow table attacks**. That's where **salting** comes in.

slides.title: Salting and bcrypt 🧂
slides.content:
## bcrypt

bcrypt is a password-hashing function designed to be slow (to resist brute force) and includes a salt automatically.

- **Salt**: a random string added to the password before hashing, making identical passwords produce different hashes.
- bcrypt stores the salt within the hash string, so you don't need a separate column.

Install:
```bash
npm install bcrypt
```

slides.title: Hashing a Password
Comparing a Password

quizData.question: What is the purpose of a salt in password hashing?

options.text: To ensure two identical passwords have different hashes
To speed up the hashing process

quizData.explanation: Salts prevent rainbow table attacks and make identical passwords produce different hashes.
